name: Arunachalam Manikandan
tagline: MS CS @ University of Minnesota · Prev. Supercell, Visa Inc.
bio: I'm a Computer Science graduate student at the University of Minnesota focused on LLMs, deep learning, vision-language models, RAG, and agentic AI systems. I enjoy building end-to-end systems that move from research ideas to real products. Currently, I work as a Graduate Research Assistant developing deep learning pipelines for biomedical image segmentation — applying large vision models like SAM and ViT to whole-slide histology images on HPC clusters. Previously, I co-developed Project Paradox at Supercell's AI Innovation Lab in Helsinki — a multi-agent framework where agents maintain beliefs, emotions, memory, and dynamic planning. I'm a frequent hackathon participant and co-creator of MinneDigest, an AI-powered newsroom that won the AI + Local News Hackathon and a $10,000 grant.

experience.role: Graduate Research Assistant — Medical Imaging & Informatics
bullets: Engineered an end-to-end deep-learning pipeline to detect and segment adipocytes in gigapixel H&E whole-slide images, diagnosing a fundamental failure mode where SOTA nucleus-segmentation vision transformers (CellViT++ / CellViT-SAM-H, HoVer-Net) detected 0 of ~9,000 adipocytes per slide.

Designed a parameter-efficient self-attention adapter for CellViT-SAM-H — freezing the 637M-parameter SAM-H encoder and training only a 7.9M-param zero-init gated attention module — enabling adipocyte detection without full-model retraining and reaching 0.48 F1.

Fine-tuned the HV-map decoder of the SAM-based segmentation transformer (15M of 700M params trainable) on custom ground-truth maps derived from spatial-transcriptomics centroids, reducing HV regression loss and improving detection.
Operationalized all training and inference on NVIDIA A100 GPUs via SLURM (mixed-precision, gradient accumulation), surfacing results through a FastAPI + React analytics dashboard.

Prototyped an in-platform AI assignment copilot (GitHub Copilot-style) powered by a RAG pipeline delivering stepwise hints, code assistance, and rapid in-context feedback — increasing student engagement and reducing context-switching.
Automated a unified serverless data platform orchestrating 30+ modular ETL jobs, centralizing configuration in serverless.yml and Lambda layers to cut pipeline execution time by 50%.

Built a fully configurable ETL job scheduler and validation framework on DynamoDB- and Lambda-backed metadata, supporting dynamic cron scheduling, real-time job toggling, and batch testing via a web control plane.
Selected as one of 16 innovators worldwide for Supercell's pilot AI Innovation Lab in Helsinki.
Co-developed Project Paradox — a Multi-Agent LLM framework for intelligent, emotionally-driven game agents with memory, beliefs, personality, and autonomous planning. Accepted at WiML @ ICML 2026.
Presented to Supercell CEO Ilkka Paananen and a panel of game industry founders including co-founders of Crunchyroll and Kabam.
Built a production-grade ElevenLabs voice agent on Google Cloud Run (FastAPI + Zapier MCP) autonomously orchestrating Google Workspace workflows, reducing end-to-end latency by 70%.
Designed Cloud Run-native tools for retrieval, communication, and scheduling, reducing manual workflows and deployment effort by 40%.

experience.company: University of Minnesota — School of Public Health
bullets: Developed Java-based simulation models for dual-stain cancer screening, driving research on early detection and patient outcome optimization.
Built an interactive Streamlit dashboard analyzing 15 HPV screening strategies across 100+ age-stratified cohorts, processing 1.5M+ simulated patient-years.

experience.company: University of Minnesota — Carlson School of Management
experience.role: Graduate Teaching Assistant — Big Data Analytics (MSBA 6331)
bullets: Assisted in teaching Big Data Analytics covering Hadoop, Hive, Spark, AWS, and scalable data pipeline design.
Designed and graded assignments and lab tasks; provided individualized support bridging conceptual learning with hands-on cloud implementation.
Built a high-performance Spark/Scala application performing daily validations on 15M+ records, identifying critical data discrepancies across large-scale financial tables.
Improved enterprise data integrity by engineering custom validation logic and automating quality checks within Visa's infrastructure.
Designed and deployed an AI-powered chatbot to streamline the U.S. visa application process using Microsoft LUIS, Azure, and QnA Maker.
Implemented NLU-based conversational flows with a custom knowledge base to enhance response accuracy and user engagement.
Led a team of 40 in organizing 25+ events; ran C.O.D.E, a placement training program with 330+ participants, officially approved by the Centre for University-Industry Collaboration.
Organized CodHer'24, a 24-hour women-only hackathon with 330+ participants and a Rs 40,000 prize pool.

projects.name: Project Paradox
projects.description: A Multi-Agent LLM framework for intelligent, emotionally-driven game agents. Built at Supercell's AI Innovation Lab. Features RAG-based memory, emotion-belief-personality modeling, and a Dynamic Story Trigger System. Accepted at WiML @ ICML 2026.

projects.name: MinneDigest
projects.description: AI-powered news-to-audio platform built in under 8 hours at a hackathon. Delivers real-time, multilingual news digests and AI-generated podcasts. Won a $10,000 grant from Hacks/Hackers and the University of Minnesota.

projects.name: Sirius — On-Device Health AI
projects.description: Privacy-first health AI built in 24 hours at the O1 Summit Hackathon. Runs Gemma E2B entirely on-device with on-device voice, camera OCR, and peer-to-peer sync between iPhone and Mac. Zero data leaves the device.

projects.name: Multimodal Sarcasm Detection via Vision-Language Models
projects.description: Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL-7B with a novel reasoning-augmented corpus, achieving 85.7% accuracy and 0.81 F1 on the MMSD dataset — outperforming Gemini 2.0 Flash on explainability. Reduced inference time from ~10 min to under 2 min via 4-bit NF4 quantization.

projects.name: ARCHITECT — Generative AI for Architectural Design
projects.description: B.Tech thesis. AI-driven house layout generation (GCN + MLP), interior remodeling visualization (Neural Style Transfer), facade generation (CycleGANs), and 3D floorplan vectorization. Validated by the HoD of the School of Architecture and Planning.

education.notes: Coursework: AI, Machine Learning, NLP, LLMs, Advanced ML, UI Design, Interactive Web
Chairperson, ACM Student Chapter of CEG

awards.title: ITrix 2023 Hackathon Winner
awards.description: First prize for Linguica, an adaptive AI learning platform built in 8 hours.

awards.title: NSF National I-Corps Program
awards.description: Selected for the NSF I-Corps entrepreneurship training program.

Remote Health Monitoring for Empowering Patients with Intelligent Wearables
